---
title: "UIAO Modernization Master Plan — Executive Brief"
subtitle: "One-page condensed brief for CIO / CISO / AO sign-off"
doc-type: executive-brief
audience: "CIO · CISO · Authorizing Official (AO) · 3PAO · Program Sponsor"
derived-from: "uiao master plan series"
upstream:
  - "phase-0-planning.qmd (full series)"
  - "phase-1-architecture.qmd"
  - "boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd"
  - "compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd"
  - "90-day-execution-plan.qmd"
date: 2026-04-26
---

# UIAO Modernization Master Plan — Executive Brief {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Series companion
This brief condenses the [five-document master plan](index.qmd) into a
single decision-ready summary. For depth, follow the inline links to the
governing document and section.
:::

---

## Mission in One Paragraph

The Unified Identity-Addressing-Overlay (UIAO) is the canonical substrate
that binds identity, device, session, routing, security, evidence, and
compliance into a single governed fabric. Modernization stabilizes
identity across hybrid directories, restores end-to-end signal
visibility, enables Zero Trust enforcement anchored to canonical state,
and establishes continuous Authority to Operate so that compliance
evidence is a by-product of normal operations rather than an after-the-
fact collection exercise. **The program is an alignment with existing
federal mandates, not a net-new initiative.**
([Phase 0 §1](phase-0-planning.qmd#executive-summary))

---

## The Structural Dependency

A complete modernization is gated by the **Boundary Gap**: the current
authorization boundary does not preserve a coherent set of six signal
classes (identity, location, session, telemetry, media, policy) as
traffic crosses cloud, WAN, and perimeter boundaries. Every downstream
plane — Security, Evidence, Compliance, Continuous ATO — operates with
attenuated signals until the boundary is modernized. The Boundary Gap
is **architectural, not organizational**, and is treated as a
prerequisite. ([Phase 0 §4](phase-0-planning.qmd#fedramp-boundary-gap-architectural-dependency))

---

## The Plan in Five Documents

| # | Document | What it produces |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | [Phase 0 — Planning](phase-0-planning.qmd) | Scope, ten planes, Boundary Gap framing, objectives, deliverables, six foundational risks |
| 2 | [Phase 1 — Architecture](phase-1-architecture.qmd) | Plane model with four-tier layering, nine canonical interfaces, eight-class signal taxonomy, control bindings, eight conformance criteria |
| 3 | [Boundary Modernization Appendix](boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd) | Six-class boundary taxonomy, continuity matrix, five-layer Signal Restoration Framework, sequencing constraints |
| 4 | [Compliance & ATO Alignment](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd) | Mandate landscape, control-binding pipeline, three-tier KSI rule, six-property evidence chain, four-mechanism cATO model |
| 5 | [90-Day Execution Plan](90-day-execution-plan.qmd) | Three thirty-day windows with closing conformance gates, five cross-cutting workstreams, exit criteria into sustained cATO |

---

## Federal Alignment

| Mandate | Alignment surface |
|---|---|
| **EO 14028** | Zero Trust, EDR, logging maturity, supply-chain integrity |
| **OMB M-22-09 / NIST SP 800-207** | Federal Zero Trust architecture; identity and device conditions of access |
| **NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 / FedRAMP Rev 5** | Control catalog, baseline tailoring, continuous monitoring |
| **CISA SCuBA** | Configuration baselines for M365 and cloud business apps |
| **OMB M-24-15** | Continuous monitoring and ongoing authorization |
| **NIST OSCAL** | Machine-readable SSP, SAR, POA&M, profile, and component artifacts |

Full mandate-to-plane crosswalk:
[Compliance §1](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#mandate-landscape).

---

## The 90-Day Window

| Days | Theme | Closing Gate |
|---|---|---|
| **1–30** | Substrate & Discovery — stand up the four substrate planes; inventory all six boundary classes; ingest the canonical control library | Substrate-conforming ([Phase 1 §7](phase-1-architecture.qmd#conformance-criteria) criteria 1–4) |
| **31–60** | Capture & Binding — SRF L4 capture at every crossing; bind controls to planes; stand up Drift Detection and queryable evidence graph | Evidence-conforming ([Compliance §7](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#conformance-criteria) criteria 1–4) |
| **61–90** | Conformance & cATO Posture — SRF L1/L2/L3/L5 deployed; OSCAL artifact set regenerable; cATO authorization-state derivation live; AO surface stood up; 3PAO reproduces an authorization-state value from the chain alone | Authorization-conforming (Phase 1 + Boundary + Compliance §7) |

Sequencing is non-negotiable: schedule pressure shifts windows, never
relaxes gates. ([90-Day §1](90-day-execution-plan.qmd#sequencing-principles))

---

## Exit Criterion

**Day 90 closes when, and only when:** Phase 1 §7, Boundary §6, and
Compliance §7 conformance criterion sets are *all* satisfied; the AO
has accepted the authorization-state derivation rule and the AO surface;
and a 3PAO has reproduced at least one authorization-state value from
the evidence chain alone. The deliverable is the **sustained operating
posture**, not the 90-day window itself. ([90-Day §9](90-day-execution-plan.qmd#exit-criteria-and-hand-off))

---

## What the AO is Asked to Decide

| # | Decision | Required Inputs |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Ratify the Phase 0 baseline | Phase 0 in full |
| 2 | Ratify the Phase 1 architecture | Phase 0 + Phase 1 |
| 3 | Ratify the boundary redesign and SRF | Phase 1 + Boundary Appendix |
| 4 | Accept the cATO operating model and authorization-state derivation rule | Compliance §5 + §7 |
| 5 | Authorize entry into the 90-day execution window | All four upstream documents ratified |
| 6 | At Day 90: accept sustained cATO posture | Day-90 conformance gate evidence + 3PAO reproducibility audit |

Decisions 1–5 are sequencing gates; decision 6 is the program's
deliverable.

---

## What the Program Will Not Do

- Select vendors or commit to specific adapters in Phase 0 or Phase 1.
- Treat compliance evidence as narrative; closure-by-narrative is
  forbidden architecturally ([Compliance §4.2](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#chain-properties), §6.2).
- Allow downstream commitment against an unratified upstream baseline;
  this rule is restated in every document's hand-off section.
- Attribute the Boundary Gap, signal loss, or visibility gaps to
  individuals or teams; framing throughout is architectural.

---

## Reading Path by Audience

| If you are… | Read |
|---|---|
| **CIO / Program Sponsor** | This brief; Phase 0 §1, §5, §6; 90-Day §9 |
| **CISO / ISSO** | This brief; Phase 0 in full; Phase 1 §2, §5, §7; Boundary §3, §6 |
| **AO** | This brief; Phase 0 §1, §4; Compliance §5, §7; 90-Day §5.3 |
| **3PAO** | Phase 1 §7; Boundary §6; Compliance §4, §5.4, §7 |
| **Engineering Leads** | Phase 1 §2–§7; Boundary §3, §4; 90-Day §3, §4, §5 |

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Brief Status
This Executive Brief is a derived consumer of the master plan series. It
is regenerated, not authored, when an upstream document is revised under
the canon-change protocol. Substantive divergence between this brief and
an upstream document is brief drift, not an upstream change.
:::
